title: acetosa ambigua
intro: Acetosa ambigua, also known as wood sorrel, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It belongs to the oxalis genus and is characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and small white or pink flowers. The leaves and stems of Acetosa ambigua have been used in herbal medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. Acetosa ambigua is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function.
